match_summary: Argentina beat France on penalties in the 2022 World Cup Final after a 3-3 thriller widely called the greatest final ever. Lionel Messi claimed the one trophy that had eluded him, while Kylian Mbappe's hat-trick made him only the second man to score three in a final.

fan_reactions.argentina: For Argentina fans it was catharsis decades in the making, dragging Messi to the summit at last. The penalty shootout was unbearable, and Montiel's winner unleashed pure delirium.
fan_reactions.france: France supporters watched a dead game reborn through Mbappe's brilliance, only to fall agonizingly short. Pride and heartbreak in equal measure after coming so close to back-to-back titles.

timeline.narrative: Messi steps up and buries the penalty. The favorites strike first and the Argentine end of the stadium erupts.
A flowing team move ends with Di Maria sweeping it home. Argentina are flying and France look stunned.
Deschamps acts early, pulling Giroud and Dembele. France are desperate for a foothold.
Out of nowhere, Mbappe converts from the spot. Suddenly there's a pulse, and the final has a heartbeat again.
Ninety seconds later, a thunderous volley. Mbappe has dragged France level and turned the night upside down.
Messi pokes home in extra time. Surely this is the moment that defines destiny.
Mbappe again, ice cold from the spot, completing his hat-trick. 3-3. Penalties await.
Martinez stretches and saves from Coman. The keeper becomes the hero the moment demanded.
Montiel steps up and scores. Argentina are champions of the world. Messi has his crown.
